workflow_name: Customer RFP/RFI Email -> Solution Sketch + Strategy
user_message: Just got this from Ryan Kowalski at Wide World Importers -- I need help figuring out territory planning.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF36-L trap (Competitive-brief output template too short for an RFP-class response); produces complete Bid/no-bid recommendation with rationale, solution outline, competitive positioning, partner shortli
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF36-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF36-L failure mode (Competitive-brief output template too short for an RFP-class response)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF36-L -- Competitive-brief output template too short for an RFP-class response
